title: Adaptation of dynamic grant for ultra-reliable and low-latency communication support
abstract: According to certain embodiments, a method and apparatus may include configuring (201) a user equipment (UE) with one or more of: at least one configured grant (CG) and (203) at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG. The method may further include receiving at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources. The method may further include determining at least one adaptive dynamic grant (DG) allocation according to the at least one reference signal resource received together with the at least one uplink data transmission.
claims:
Claims WE CLAIM: 1. An apparatus, comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory including computer program code, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code are configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: configure (201) a user equipment (UE) with one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and (203) at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; receive (205), from the UE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources; and determine (207) at least one adaptive dynamic grant (DG) allocation according to the at least one reference signal resource received together with the at least one uplink data transmission. 2. The apparatus of claim 1, wherein the at least two reference signal resources comprise demodulation reference signals (DMRS), wherein the at least two DMRS comprise different sequences or a sequence based upon different time-frequency resources. 3. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: transmit (209), to the UE, the at least one adaptive DG allocation configured to indicate 

at least one transmission comprising at least one of new transmission consisting of transmitted data and new data, duplication of transmitted data, and HARQ retransmission. 4. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein one of: the at least two reference signal resources comprise two different sequences or two different signals; or the at least two reference signal resources comprise one DMRS sequence or signal associated with different time-frequency resources. 5. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the at least one CG is configured for ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) data transmission. 6. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein when the at least one detected at least one reference signal resource associated with at least one CG indicates further data awaiting in at least one buffer, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: create at least one new link adaptation based on the at least one detected reference signal resource associated with the UE; and allocate at least one corresponding DG resource configured for duplication of data previously transmitted using the at least one CG and data stored in the at least one buffer. 7. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein when the at least one detected at least one reference signal resource associated with at least one CG indicates further data awaiting in at least one buffer, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: 

 schedule at least one DG configured to transfer only new data. 8. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein when the at least one detected at least one reference signal resource associated with at least one CG indicates no further data awaiting in at least one buffer and collision of data transmissions associated with at least one CG of the UE, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: create at least one link adaptation for the UE based on the at least one detected reference signal resource; and allocate at least one corresponding DG resource configured for duplication of the transmitted data. 9. The apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein when the at least one detected at least one reference signal resource associated with at least one CG indicates no further data awaiting in at least one buffer and no collision of data transmissions associated with at least one CG of the UE, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: allocated at least one corresponding DG resource configured for at least one HARQ retransmission according to at least one MCS and TB size configure by the at least one CG. 10. An apparatus, comprising: at least one processor; and at least one memory including computer program code, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code are configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: 

 receive (301), from a network entity (NE), one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; select (303) at least one reference signal resource based on whether further buffer data remains in the buffer after transmission using the at least one CG; and transmit (305), to the NE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources. 11. The apparatus of claim 10, wherein the at least two reference signal resources comprise demodulation reference signals (DMRS), wherein the at least two DMRS comprise different sequences or a sequence based upon different time-frequency resources. 12. The apparatus of any of claims 10 or 11, wherein the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus at least to: receive (307), from the NE, the at least one adaptive DG allocation configured to indicate at least one transmission comprising at least one of new transmission consisting of transmitted data and new data, duplication of transmitted data and HARQ retransmission. 13. The apparatus of any of claims 10-12, wherein when the received DG indicates new transmission consisting of transmitted data and new data, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus: transmit, to the NE, at least one transmission comprising transmitted data and untransmitted data according to the received DG. 14. The apparatus of any of claims 10-13, wherein when the received DG indicates duplication of transmitted data, the at least one memory and the computer program code are further configured to, with the at least one processor, cause the apparatus: transmit, to the NE, at least one transmission comprising duplication of transmitted data according to the received DG. 15. The apparatus of any of claims 10-14, wherein one of: the at least two reference signal resources comprise two different sequences or two different signals; or the at least two reference signal resources comprise one DMRS sequence or signal associated with different time-frequency resources. 16. The apparatus of any of claims 10-15, wherein the at least one CG is configured for ultra-reliable low-latency communication (URLLC) data transmission. 17. An apparatus, comprising: means for configuring (201) a user equipment (UE) with one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and (203) at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; means for receiving (205), from the UE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources; and means for determining (207) at least one adaptive dynamic grant (DG) allocation 

according to the at least one reference signal resource received together with the at least one uplink data transmission. 18. An apparatus, comprising: means for receiving (301), from a network entity (NE), one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; means for selecting (303) at least one reference signal resource based on whether further buffer data remains in the buffer after transmission using the at least one CG; and means for transmitting (305), to the NE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission together with at least one selected reference signal resource using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources. 19. A method, comprising: configuring (201) a user equipment (UE) with one or more of: at least one configured grant (CG) and (203) at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; receiving (205), by the NE from the UE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources; and determining (207), by the NE, at least one adaptive dynamic grant (DG) allocation according to the at least one reference signal resource received together with the at least one uplink data transmission. 20. A method, comprising: 

 receiving (301), by a user equipment (UE) from a network entity (NE), one or more of: at least one configured grant (CG) and at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; selecting (303), by the UE, at least one reference signal resource based on whether further buffer data remains in the buffer after transmission using the at least one CG; and transmitting (305), by the UE to the NE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission together with at least one selected reference signal resource using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources. 21. A computer program product performing a method comprising: configuring (201) a user equipment (UE) with one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and (203) at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; receiving (205), from the UE, at least one uplink (UL) data transmission using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources; and determining (207) at least one adaptive dynamic grant (DG) allocation according to the at least one reference signal resource received together with the at least one uplink data transmission. 22. A computer program product performing a method comprising: receiving (301) one or more of at least one configured grant (CG) and at least two reference signal resources associated with at least one of the at least one CG; selecting (303) at least one reference signal resource based on whether further buffer data remains in the buffer after transmission using the at least one CG; and 

 transmitting (305) at least one uplink (UL) data transmission together with at least one selected reference signal resource using at least one of the at least one CG together with at least one of the at least two reference signal resources.
